event.name: NHL — Maple Leafs vs Boston Bruins (March 2026)

context.pre_match_notes: Morning skate report: Bruins starting goaltender did NOT skate (stayed off ice). NHL morning skate is the key pre-game signal for goaltender status. Backup goaltender was the one skating. SportMind morning skate protocol: if starter does not skate = starter likely playing (resting); if backup skates without starter = backup likely starting. This is counter-intuitive — resting is normal for starters.

question: Interpret the morning skate signal. Does starter absence at morning skate mean starter is NOT playing?

verified_outcome.key_variable: morning_skate_signal — starter absence = starter RESTING (normal); starter played; backup skate = backup starting (if reversed)
